frames.name: Civil Rights/Police Overreach Frame
frames.description: Presents Rahman's arrest as excessive force against a disabled activist, emphasizing her silence and peaceful nature of her action
frames.emphasis: Rahman's disability, her silence during the incident, physical injuries from arrest, and pattern of law enforcement overreach

frames.name: Congressional Disruption/Rule Violation Frame
frames.description: Focuses on Capitol Police enforcement of established rules prohibiting demonstrations during State of the Union
frames.emphasis: Clear prohibition on demonstrations, Rahman's refusal to comply with lawful orders, established protocols

frames.name: Political Accountability Frame
frames.description: Centers on Rep. Omar's response and calls for investigation into the incident
frames.emphasis: Omar's condemnation, demands for investigation, implications for democratic norms

frames.name: Broader Immigration Enforcement Context Frame
frames.description: Connects the State of the Union incident to Rahman's previous encounter with ICE agents in Minneapolis
frames.emphasis: Pattern of aggressive law enforcement, Rahman's status as ICE encounter victim, broader immigration policy context

outlierPerspectives: Rahman's perspective that standing during applause should be permitted since 'there are only two things you can do at the State of the Union, and they are sit down and stand up'
Rahman's framing of her attendance as educational rather than activist: seeking to 'understand the texture' of politics as someone outside policy work
missingContext: Specific Capitol Police protocols for handling disruptions during State of the Union addresses
Historical precedent for arrests during State of the Union speeches
Details about Rahman's legal status following the ICE encounter in Minneapolis
Explanation of how guest invitations and security screening work for State of the Union
Medical documentation of Rahman's injuries and disability claims
Full text of what Trump said during the moment Rahman stood up
